title: router.c: Dangling pointer risk - packet_data not copied despite header claiming copy
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/router.c
Lines: 331, 666

In lichen_router_queue_pending() line 331:
    slot->packet_data = (uint8_t *)packet_data;  /* Note: caller owns data */

In lichen_router_dtn_buffer() line 666:
    slot->data = (uint8_t *)data;  /* Note: caller owns data */

The header file router.h says 'copied' in the documentation:
- Line 326: '@param packet_data Packet data to queue (copied).'
- Line 441: '@param data Message data (copied).'

But the implementation does NOT copy the data - it stores the raw pointer. If the caller frees or reuses the buffer, these become dangling pointers leading to use-after-free bugs.

Either the implementation should actually copy the data, or the header documentation must be corrected to reflect the actual ownership semantics.
close_reason: Fixed use-after-free vulnerability: lichen_router_queue_pending and lichen_router_dtn_buffer now copy data into static buffers instead of storing raw pointers. Added CONFIG_LICHEN_ROUTER_MAX_PENDING_PACKET_SIZE (256) and CONFIG_LICHEN_ROUTER_DTN_MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE (512) configs. Header documentation now matches implementation. Added SECURITY comments explaining the fix.
